---
name: ui-ux-design
description: >
  UI/UX design principles for user interfaces. Visibility, affordance, cognitive load, accessibility, progressive disclosure, performance perception. USE FOR: building UI, designing interface, frontend, UX, accessibility, a11y, form design, navigation, error states, loading states.
---

# UI/UX Design Principles

## Feedback & Communication

- **Visibility** — show system state; never leave users in dark
- **Real-world language** — user vocabulary, not jargon
- **Affordance** — interactive elements look interactive

## Control & Trust

- **User control** — escape/undo always; never trap
- **Error prevention** — guide away from mistakes
- **Consistency** — same patterns across surfaces

## Cognitive Load

- **Recognition > recall** — surface options; minimize memory load
- **Progressive disclosure** — reveal complexity gradually
- **Minimalism** — only what serves user now

## Inclusivity & Performance

- **a11y** — keyboard, screen reader, contrast, semantic HTML
- **Perceived speed** — feedback beats actual speed
- **Flexibility** — novice/expert paths

## Checklist

- [ ] Loading/error/success states visible?
- [ ] Escape/undo available?
- [ ] Labels consistent?
- [ ] Keyboard navigable?
- [ ] WCAG AA contrast?
- [ ] No unnecessary elements?
